title: How did Kaworu become so popular
display_title: How did Kaworu become so popular
wikitext:
[]: How did Kaworu become so popular?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Nov 18, 2009 5:51 pm This is a character that got as much screentime as Jet Alone. But from the merchandising, there would be no way you'd think that. For such a minor character, he sure has gotten a lot of emphasis placed on him with the manga, GoSII, and now, Rebuild. So what exactly did people see in him? I guess I would know, since when I first read about Kaworu, I was like, "I know I'd like him." I guess his calm demeanor is really appealing, as well as self-sacrifice and trust stuff. It must be universally liked.

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Wed Nov 18, 2009 5:56 pm i think hopeless, obese yaoi fangirls make up a large percentage of fans. i like him though, one of my favourite characters. i have reasons why i like him but i can't be bothered doing a mini psycho-analysis in order to list them

[]:             Quote Post by Natsuka_Chie » Wed Nov 18, 2009 5:58 pm He's mysterious, hot, and makes Shinji look like a shy young girl.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Nov 18, 2009 6:06 pm I have no idea. But the above post makes Mongoosdog's theory possibly true...

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Nov 18, 2009 6:12 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I have no idea. But Mongoosdog's theory is true...

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Nov 18, 2009 6:13 pm I really love Sadamoto's take on Kaworu, but the series iteration of him didn't leave as much of an impression on me. I think manga Kaworu is interesting because of his amorality. He's not deliberately malicious, but basic human emotions are completely foreign to him, and not in a cute Mr. Spock sort of way. He thinks nothing of snapping a kitten's neck or calling Rei a "fool" after she kills herself to save Shinji. He wants to use Shinji to feel human emotions, but they aren't forthcoming. When he dies, he's nearly as alien as he was when he was introduced.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Nov 18, 2009 9:26 pm I'm sure people like Kaworu for multiple reasons, and not just because he'd make a yaoi manga with Shinji. Hopefully. But I did get a feeling that Anno wanted us to think of Kaworu as enchanting. He did keep smiling all the damn time. I'll wait for the harder hitting people to jump in here.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Nov 18, 2009 9:50 pm Kaworu's bizzare wierdness is probably what attracts people's attention. He's just so abnormal.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:01 pm Just my take, but Kaworu stands out from the rest of the Angels because he passes as one of us, so we the viewer relate to him more than the others, since they at best only look vaguely human. Plus, he's pretty damn cute, so couple that with his mysteriousness...

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:13 pm Well to start of it does have to do with the ambiguity that surrounds, Kaworu, he also was a novel way to characterise the angels. Natsuka_Chie wrote: He's mysterious, hot, and makes Shinji look like a shy young girl.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:21 pm Yes Shin/Tabby certainly does have a good deal of Yaoi potential IMO. BTW, Synaps, those pics are they from fanfics/doujins/products somewhere? EDIT: They look like they might come from SIRP?

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:22 pm how cute, though the Ritsuko one just comes out of nowhere

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:31 pm Kaworu embodies unconditional love and acceptance. I don't know if that's what made him popular, but that's why I like him.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:40 pm Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: Yes Shin/Tabby certainly does have a good deal of Yaoi potential IMO. BTW, Synaps, those pics http://i528.photobucket.com/albums/dd325/Synapsidia/EVA/12122807003591.jpg are they from fanfics/doujins/products somewhere? EDIT: They look like they might come from SIRP?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Nov 18, 2009 11:26 pm Can you help me cut out the Ritsuko/Shinji part and make fit the parameters for an Avvie? EIDT: Ill use it later I decided to stick with the Shinjiquarium again

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Nov 19, 2009 12:03 am My theory about Kaworu's popularity is the same one behind Rei's popularity; these characters - especially compared with the others in the show - are mysterious, blank canvasses on which the audience can project all of their fantasies on and, essentially, "fill in the blanks". It's not that they aren't given adequate characterization (especially Rei), but they are certainly much more abstract and symbolic than the rest of the cast. Plus, there's the whole sexually ambiguous nature behind both of them which definitely appeals to horny anime fans and fanfic writers.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Thu Nov 19, 2009 12:16 am @Shinji Give me a little while, and I'll have it done (I'm probably sick but the idea of Ritsuko molesting Shinji to get revenge against Gendo is a rather interesting one) , but this thread is supposed to be about our enigmatic cat boy (the silver one of course), and why everyone loves him [URL=http://s528.photobucket.com/albums/dd325/Synapsidia/EVA/?action=view&current=Kaworu_prototype1.png] [/URL] (Shito no Nyanko-kun?) @Eva Yojimbo And what we do find of his background has an apeal to some fans.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Nov 19, 2009 12:07 pm Lucretius wrote: I really love Sadamoto's take on Kaworu, but the series iteration of him didn't leave as much of an impression on me. I think manga Kaworu is interesting because of his amorality. He's not deliberately malicious, but basic human emotions are completely foreign to him, and not in a cute Mr. Spock sort of way. He thinks nothing of snapping a kitten's neck or calling Rei a "fool" after she kills herself to save Shinji. He wants to use Shinji to feel human emotions, but they aren't forthcoming. When he dies, he's nearly as alien as he was when he was introduced.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Thu Nov 19, 2009 1:32 pm Xard wrote: Whole thing was from very start was enormous shitting project on yaoi fans. it was pretty hilarious

[]:             Quote Post by Boredom » Thu Nov 19, 2009 1:51 pm I went to a couple of stores, I was disapoint at the lack of Kaworu figures. but that's just me
